moves.name: Close the gap
moves.note: Cover your face with both arms as you step in. Make safe contact, trap or control the nearest arm, then work toward T-position, pummel, or a basic clinch so you are not trading strikes at distance.

moves.name: Sucker Punch Defense
moves.note: Keep your hands up and open. For a wide punch, cover close to your head and close distance; for a straight or lunging punch, redirect it across your body and move off the line.

moves.name: Front Kick Defense (to mid-section)
moves.note: Soften the impact by bracing and stepping back. Catch the kicking leg when it is safe, cup the heel, lift the leg, and create space or off-balance them enough to leave.

moves.name: Low Kick Defense
moves.note: Lift your checking leg so the shin takes the kick instead of your planted leg. Keep the foot flexed, knee pointed out, hands up, and put the foot back down with balance.

moves.name: T-Position Hip Throw
moves.note: From T-position, step across so your feet land between theirs. Turn your hips across their body, keep close contact, load their weight onto your hip, and finish slowly in practice.

moves.name: Wrist Grab Escape (same side)
moves.note: Make a stable base first. Rotate your wrist toward the gap between their thumb and fingers, then pull your elbow back to your body and step away.

moves.name: Wrist Grab Escape (two handed)
moves.note: Make base and keep your shoulders down. Reach over to grab your own trapped wrist, pull upward through the weak point between their thumbs, then reset your distance.

moves.name: Single-Hand Lapel Grab Defense
moves.note: For the lapel grab from behind, secure their wrist with your thumbs down. Step through and turn your body to put pressure on the elbow, using control only as much as needed to escape.

moves.name: Single-Hand Lapel Grab Defense (bent arm)
moves.note: Control their wrist and elbow so the arm stays bent like an L. Step under the arm, move behind them, then bring your head out and settle into the chicken-wing control.

moves.name: Single-Hand Lapel Grab Defense (palm turned up)
moves.note: Against the palm-up lapel grab, use a two-hand grip on their wrist. Step forward, keep their hand close to your chest, and press down into the wrist lock; add a trip only if trained and necessary.

moves.name: Two-Handed Lapel Grab Defense (with hip throw)
moves.note: Thread one elbow up through their arms and wrap around the head or upper body. Step in close, turn your hips across them, and use the hip throw mechanics rather than pulling with your arms.

moves.name: Two-Handed Front Choke Defense
moves.note: Protect your airway first. Pin or block their elbows, step to block one leg, duck your head under the arm, and step back to create space.

moves.name: Single Hand Front Choke Defense (against wall)
moves.note: Against the wall, clasp your hands and strike down through the choking wrist to loosen it. Turn off the wall, keep your balance, and use the elbow strike only as a trained escape tool.

moves.name: Standing Hair Grab Defense
moves.note: Clamp their grabbing hand tightly to your head so the hair is not pulled more. Step forward to take slack out of the grip, then turn into the wrist lock and move to safety.

moves.name: Standing Ear Grab Defense
moves.note: Clamp their hand to your ear to reduce damage. Secure their wrist, pull it close, and turn your body to apply elbow pressure carefully while you escape.

moves.name: Headlock Defense (attacker upright)
moves.note: Reach up to cup the chin, look up to protect your neck, and drop your weight. Lift or block the near leg, then walk them backward and down with control.

moves.name: Headlock Defense (with punch)
moves.note: Shoot both hands forward to block the punching arm. Grip the meaty part of their hand, turn it away, look up to make space at your neck, take two steps back, and duck your head out.

moves.name: Rear Bear Hug Defense (over the arms)
moves.note: Make a strong base right away: feet wide, knees bent, hips heavy. Step around their legs and move backward so they lose balance, then separate once you can.

moves.name: Rear Bear Hug Defense (under the arms with lift)
moves.note: If they lift you from behind with your arms free, hook the outside of one leg with your foot. Drop your weight forward, pull the leg up, and use your hips to make them put you down.

moves.name: Front Bear Hug Defense (over the arms)
moves.note: Make base before they can lift or squeeze. Put your hands on their hips, arch your hips back to make space, then move to T-position and use a hip throw if trained.

moves.name: Front Bear Hug Defense (under the arms with frame)
moves.note: Make base with your feet wide and hips back. Build a frame with your hands against the face or upper chest, push to create space, and leave as soon as the grip opens.
